summary: VibeDeck Phase 2 testing. Backend: 22/22 pytest tests pass (health, models, auth-401, /me admin+user, scratch validation, share toggle round-trip, public share read, 404 when not public, per-slide edit out-of-range / empty / SUCCESS preserves id+accent and leaves other slides untouched, admin overview/users/generations/non-admin 403/set-tier success+invalid, ownership scoping, theme palette propagation on fresh sunset/light scratch generation). One non-functional issue: whole-deck edit endpoint completes server-side but the Kubernetes/Cloudflare ingress returns 502 after ~60s (k2-think-v2 regenerates ALL slides serially in edit_deck; takes longer than the proxy's 60s read timeout) — the DB update still lands and theme is preserved, but the client sees a 502 page. Frontend: every required testid is wired and the full demo flow works — landing hero-input -> deck-setup-modal (5 tones, 5 palettes, 2 slide-modes), anon -> /sign-in redirect, demo sign-in -> /dashboard, Start-from-scratch modal exposes scratch-topic-input + theme-picker + confirm-scratch-button, Editor /p/{id} shows chat-panel with 'Editing slide N' label that updates when next-slide clicked (counter 1/9 -> 2/9, label 'Editing slide 1' -> 'Editing slide 2'), all controls (prev/next/play/counter/thumbnail-strip/chat-input/chat-send/editor-share) are visible, share-popover reveals share-link with /present/{shareId} format + share-copy, Present /present/{shareId} renders with present-canvas+controls without auth, Pricing shows 3 tier cards with 'Your plan' badge on Free for the free user, Admin /admin shows users table (14 rows, tier-select dropdowns) and admin-tab-generations; non-admin sees 'Admin access required'. Global theme-toggle flips dark class on <html> AND persists across reload. P0 VISUAL ISSUE: BOTH Editor and Present canvases have child elements overflowing the 16:9 frame (Editor: 9 overflows, Present: 8 overflows on the k2tester/BrewBuddy deck) — e.g. the hero title '☕️ YOUR DESK’S NEW BEST FRIEND' spills outside the slide-canvas / present-canvas bounding box.

minor.issue: Whole-deck edit with k2-think-v2 commonly exceeds the ingress proxy's ~60s response timeout, so the client receives a 502 HTML page even though the server completes the update (theme preserved, messages appended). Either offload to a background task with polling (like /scratch) or stream/chunk the response.

issues: P0: slide content overflows the 16:9 canvas. JS bounding-box scan finds 9 elements outside slide-canvas (Editor) and 8 outside present-canvas (Present). Visible offender: the hero text “☕️ YOUR DESK’S NEW BEST FRIEND”. Likely a SlideRenderer layout issue at narrow heights (titles word-wrap then exceed the cqh-driven canvas height). Repro: sign in as k2tester, open /p/96fe2627693d4552a38945d2b444d654 (or /present/338103f47d754b92be2ffa31b0a5b43a).

action_items: Fix P0 16:9 canvas overflow in SlideRenderer for the existing k2tester light/sunset deck (Editor + Present). Add overflow:hidden + min-w-0 to slide content roots, clamp hero title font-size with cqi/cqh, and verify no positioned element extends past the canvas rect.
Make POST /api/presentations/{id}/edit non-blocking (background task + poll status, same pattern as /scratch) so the ingress doesn't 502 on long k2-think-v2 regenerations.

Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md: the demo-id for k2tester@test.vibedeck would normalize to demo_k2testertestvibedeck, NOT demo_k2tester. Either change the credential email to one that normalizes to k2tester (e.g. just 'k2tester' — but the sign-in form requires @) or instruct testers to seed localStorage directly with id=demo_k2tester.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: edit_presentation is fully sync/awaited and can run 60-180s with k2-think-v2 — always behind ingress 60s timeout. Mirror /scratch background-task pattern.
pipeline.py edit_deck regenerates every slide; consider editing only impacted slides or diffing the outline first to cut latency.

SlideRenderer: containerType:size + width:min(100%, calc(100cqh*16/9)) gives correct aspect, but inner content (titles/images) isn’t clamped — add `overflow:hidden` on the canvas wrapper and font-size clamps (text-[clamp(...)]) so headers don’t spill.

frontend/src/lib/auth.js demo-id derivation strips non-alnum, but the sign-in form’s HTML5 email validation requires '@', so it’s impossible to type an email that produces some seeded demo ids (e.g. demo_k2tester). Either drop the type=email or document the workaround.
server.py @app.on_event('startup') still deprecated — migrate to lifespan handler (carried over from iter 1).

success_rate.frontend: 92% — all functional + integration checks pass; P0 canvas overflow is the only failure

test_credentials: Frontend: localStorage.setItem('vibedeck_demo_user', JSON.stringify({id:'demo_k2tester', email:'k2tester@test.vibedeck'})) for the existing READY deck. Admin: same trick with id='demo_adminfalakme', email='admin@falak.me'. Backend: header X-Test-User: demo_k2tester (or demo_adminfalakme + X-Test-Email: admin@falak.me).
seed_data_creation: Test test_scratch_with_theme creates and DELETES a k2-think-v2 sunset/light deck owned by demo_other_alice_test (clean teardown). No other persistent test data added.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v` — all 22 tests pass against the live preview. Slow tests (TestSlideEdit::test_slide_edit_only_target_changed ~60s, TestScratchWithTheme ~120s) are LLM-dependent; whole-deck edit test (TestDeckEditPreservesTheme) will return 502 from the proxy even though the backend completes — keep that in mind. Use localStorage seeding for the demo_k2tester user (the sign-in form’s @ validation prevents typing the bare 'k2tester' string). The P0 canvas overflow needs visual fix in SlideRenderer; rerun the JS bounding-box scan in iteration 3 to confirm: walk children of [data-testid='slide-canvas'] / 'present-canvas' and assert none extend past its getBoundingClientRect.

bug_1_whole_deck_edit_502.root_cause: edit_presentation awaits pipeline.edit_deck synchronously. With k2-think-v2 the regeneration of all 9 slides takes ~70-120s, exceeding the Kubernetes ingress/proxy read timeout (~60s). Server still finishes (DB update lands, theme preserved), but the client gets a 502 HTML page — a UX failure for whole-deck chat.
bug_1_whole_deck_edit_502.mitigation: Mirror the /scratch background-task pattern: insert messages immediately, kick off asyncio.create_task(_run_edit(...)), return 202 with {status:'editing'}, and have the editor poll /presentations/{id}.

bug_2_canvas_overflow.root_cause: SlideRenderer’s outer wrapper has the correct cqi-based 16:9 sizing, but inner blocks (notably the hero title row in feature-grid / quote / hero layouts) don’t apply overflow:hidden or font-clamps, so once content wraps to >100cqh it spills below the canvas’s bottom edge.
bug_2_canvas_overflow.reproduction: Sign in as demo_k2tester, open /p/96fe2627693d4552a38945d2b444d654, navigate any slide; run the bounding-box scan in the test report — 9 overflows found in Editor, 8 in Present (1920x1080 viewport).
bug_2_canvas_overflow.mitigation: Add `overflow:hidden` + `min-w-0` to the slide canvas root, clamp hero text (e.g. text-[clamp(2rem,6cqi,5rem)]), and verify in both light and dark themes.
